event.name: Blue Space
event.description: Blue Space is a one-day hardware + software hackathon where students come together to build, learn, and connect. Whether you're a seasoned engineer or picking up a soldering iron for the first time, there's a place for you here.

faqs.question: Who can attend Blue Space?
faqs.answer: Any student in middle school or high school is welcome! No experience required — we have workshops for all skill levels.

faqs.question: Is it really free?
faqs.answer: 100% free. No registration fee, no hidden costs. We even provide food and materials.

faqs.question: Do I need hardware experience?
faqs.answer: Not at all! We have beginner-friendly workshops covering everything from basic circuits to PCB design. You'll leave knowing more than you came in with.

faqs.question: What should I bring?
faqs.answer: Bring your laptop, a charger, and your curiosity. We'll provide hardware components, soldering stations, and all the tools you need.

faqs.question: What are the PCB coupons?
faqs.answer: Every participant receives free PCB manufacturing coupons courtesy of EasyEDA, plus a free domain from Generation XYZ so you can launch your project after the event.

faqs.question: Can I come alone?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Most people come solo and form teams on the day. Our community vibe means you'll make friends fast.

faqs.question: Where exactly is the venue?
faqs.answer: Zoho Corporation, 4141 Hacienda Dr, Pleasanton, CA 94588. Parking is available on site.

faqs.question: How do I stay updated?
faqs.answer: Join our Discord server at discord.gg/TNjUjYkB3 for real-time announcements, team formation, and pre-event workshops.
